steps.title: Locate the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence says Dickens's novel originally had a different title, then adds that while writing and editing he had planned another title. These are two independent clauses.

steps.title: Place though correctly
steps.content: B and D create comma splices before the second independent clause. C misuses 'though' at the start of the second clause. The punctuation has to handle both the interrupting adverb and the clause boundary. A sets off the trailing 'though' and uses a semicolon to join the clauses. The answer is A.
